words.definition_vi: hiểu rõ, nắm bắt được ý nghĩa hoặc khái niệm (không phải chỉ nắm bằng tay)
words.definition_en: to understand or comprehend something fully
words.example_sentence: Many students struggle to grasp the concept of quantum mechanics in their first year.

words.definition_vi: giải quyết, xử lý (một vấn đề khó); không phải là tấn công thể chất
words.definition_en: to deal with or address a difficult problem or task
words.example_sentence: The government must tackle the rising issue of climate change with concrete policies.

words.definition_vi: theo đuổi, tiếp tục (một mục tiêu, sự nghiệp); không chỉ là đuổi theo
words.definition_en: to continue or engage in an activity or goal over time
words.example_sentence: She decided to pursue a career in medicine after years of studying biology.

words.definition_vi: nuôi dưỡng, khuyến khích phát triển (tình cảm, kỹ năng, mối quan hệ)
words.definition_en: to encourage or promote the development of something
words.example_sentence: Schools should foster critical thinking skills in students rather than just memorizing facts.

words.definition_vi: làm suy yếu, phá hoại (uy tín, niềm tin, nền tảng); không phải là đào hầm
words.definition_en: to weaken or damage something gradually or indirectly
words.example_sentence: Spreading false information can undermine public trust in scientific institutions.

words.definition_vi: thuyết phục, hấp dẫn mạnh mẽ (lý do, bằng chứng); không phải là bắt buộc
words.definition_en: powerfully interesting or convincing; demanding attention
words.example_sentence: The documentary presented compelling evidence that challenged the traditional historical narrative.

words.definition_vi: tùy tiện, không có lý do rõ ràng; không phải là độc lập
words.definition_en: based on random choice or personal whim rather than reason or system
words.example_sentence: The teacher's decision to fail half the class seemed arbitrary and unfair to the students.

words.definition_vi: vốn có, bẩm sinh (tính chất); không phải là thừa kế tài sản
words.definition_en: existing as a natural or permanent element or quality
words.example_sentence: There is an inherent risk in any investment, no matter how carefully it is planned.

words.definition_vi: mâu thuẫn, điều tưởng chừng vô lý nhưng có thể đúng
words.definition_en: a situation or statement that seems contradictory but may be true
words.example_sentence: The paradox of modern life is that we have more technology but less genuine human connection.

words.definition_vi: mơ hồ, có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách khác nhau
words.definition_en: open to more than one interpretation; unclear in meaning
words.example_sentence: The politician's statement was deliberately ambiguous to avoid offending either side of the debate.

words.definition_vi: giảm nhẹ, làm bớt nghiêm trọng (hậu quả, tác hại)
words.definition_en: to make something less severe or serious
words.example_sentence: The company implemented new safety measures to mitigate the risk of workplace accidents.

words.definition_vi: che khuất, làm mờ đi (sự thật, tầm nhìn); không phải là tối tăm
words.definition_en: to make something unclear or hidden from view
words.example_sentence: The thick fog obscured the view of the mountains in the distance.

words.definition_vi: có vẻ hợp lý, có thể tin được (nhưng chưa chắc đúng)
words.definition_en: seeming reasonable or probable; believable
words.example_sentence: While the theory is plausible, it still requires more scientific evidence to be accepted.

words.definition_vi: bền bỉ, có khả năng phục hồi sau khó khăn
words.definition_en: able to recover quickly from difficulties; tough
words.example_sentence: The resilient community rebuilt their homes after the natural disaster with remarkable speed.

words.definition_vi: sự xem xét kỹ lưỡng, kiểm tra chi tiết
words.definition_en: careful and detailed examination or inspection
words.example_sentence: The financial records came under scrutiny after the auditors discovered several discrepancies.

words.definition_vi: chứng minh, cung cấp bằng chứng cho (một khẳng định)
words.definition_en: to provide evidence or proof for a claim or statement
words.example_sentence: The researcher must substantiate her hypothesis with experimental data before publishing.

words.definition_vi: bất ổn định, dễ thay đổi (thị trường, tình hình, tính cách)
words.definition_en: liable to change rapidly and unpredictably; unstable
words.example_sentence: The stock market has been volatile this year due to economic uncertainty.

words.definition_vi: thực tế, dựa trên kết quả thực tế chứ không phải lý tưởng
words.definition_en: dealing with things in a practical, realistic way based on actual circumstances
words.example_sentence: The government took a pragmatic approach to the housing crisis by focusing on affordable solutions.

words.definition_vi: khai thác, lôi ra (thông tin, phản ứng); không phải là gợi ý
words.definition_en: to draw out or bring forth information, response, or reaction
words.example_sentence: The interviewer's questions were designed to elicit honest responses from the candidates.

words.definition_vi: vòng qua, tránh (luật lệ, vấn đề) bằng cách khéo léo
words.definition_en: to find a way around an obstacle or rule; to avoid by going around
words.example_sentence: Some companies try to circumvent environmental regulations by relocating to countries with weaker laws.

words.definition_vi: xác nhận, chứng thực (lời khai, sự kiện) bằng bằng chứng khác
words.definition_en: to confirm or support a statement or finding with additional evidence
words.example_sentence: The witness's testimony was corroborated by security camera footage from that night.

words.definition_vi: đẩy nhanh, tăng tốc độ (quá trình, công việc)
words.definition_en: to make a process or action happen faster or more quickly
words.example_sentence: The new software system will expedite the approval process for loan applications.

words.definition_vi: phân kỳ, đi theo hướng khác nhau (ý kiến, con đường, quan điểm)
words.definition_en: to separate and go in different directions; to differ in opinion or approach
words.example_sentence: The two researchers' interpretations of the data diverged significantly, leading to different conclusions.

questions.question_text: The professor's explanation was so clear that even the most complex concepts became easy to ___.
questions.explanation: Grasp = hiểu rõ, nắm bắt được ý nghĩa. Grab, hold, catch đều có nghĩa vật lý (nắm bằng tay) nhưng không phù hợp ngữ cảnh 'hiểu khái niệm'.

questions.question_text: The government announced a new initiative to _____ the problem of urban pollution through stricter regulations.
questions.explanation: Tackle = giải quyết, xử lý vấn đề khó. Address, handle, manage đều có thể dùng nhưng tackle là từ mạnh nhất, thể hiện sự quyết tâm giải quyết vấn đề lớn.

questions.question_text: After graduating, she decided to _____ a career in environmental science, dedicating herself to research.
questions.explanation: Pursue = theo đuổi, tiếp tục (mục tiêu, sự nghiệp). Chase có vẻ quá thô, follow không đủ mạnh, seek chỉ tìm kiếm chứ không theo đuổi lâu dài.

questions.question_text: The school's new mentorship program aims to _____ a love of learning in young students.
questions.explanation: Foster = nuôi dưỡng, khuyến khích phát triển (tình cảm, kỹ năng). Promote quá chung chung, inspire không chỉ sự phát triển lâu dài, encourage không đủ mạnh.

questions.question_text: Spreading misinformation can _____ public confidence in government institutions.
questions.explanation: Undermine = làm suy yếu, phá hoại (uy tín, niềm tin) từ từ. Damage quá mạnh, weaken không chỉ sự phá hoại gián tiếp, reduce không phù hợp ngữ cảnh.

questions.question_text: The documentary presented _____ evidence that forced viewers to reconsider their long-held beliefs.
questions.explanation: Compelling = thuyết phục, hấp dẫn mạnh mẽ. Persuasive chỉ thuyết phục, interesting quá yếu, convincing không đủ mạnh về sức hút.

questions.question_text: The judge criticized the decision as _____, claiming it was based on personal preference rather than legal principles.
questions.explanation: Arbitrary = tùy tiện, không có lý do rõ ràng. Random quá chung chung, capricious quá mạnh, unfair không chỉ tính tùy tiện.

questions.question_text: There is an _____ risk in any financial investment, regardless of how carefully it is analyzed.
questions.explanation: Inherent = vốn có, bẩm sinh (tính chất). Intrinsic, innate, built-in đều gần nghĩa nhưng inherent là từ phổ biến nhất trong ngữ cảnh học thuật.

questions.question_text: The _____ of modern technology is that it connects us globally yet isolates us personally.
questions.explanation: Paradox = mâu thuẫn tưởng chừng vô lý nhưng có thể đúng. Contradiction quá chung chung, inconsistency không chỉ sự mâu thuẫn logic, enigma là bí ẩn.

questions.question_text: The politician's statement was deliberately _____ so that both supporters and critics could interpret it favorably.
questions.explanation: Ambiguous = mơ hồ, có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách. Unclear quá chung chung, vague không chỉ sự đa nghĩa, confusing chỉ gây nhầm lẫn.

questions.question_text: The new safety protocols were implemented to _____ the impact of workplace accidents on employees.
questions.explanation: Mitigate = giảm nhẹ, làm bớt nghiêm trọng (hậu quả). Reduce quá chung chung, alleviate chỉ giảm đau khổ, ease không đủ chính thức.

questions.question_text: The thick fog _____ the view of the valley, making it impossible for hikers to see the trail ahead.
questions.explanation: Obscure = che khuất, làm mờ đi (tầm nhìn). Conceal quá mạnh, hide không chỉ làm mờ, clouds là động từ nhưng ít dùng trong ngữ cảnh này.
